title: 5.1: Failing tests for recent commit loading and path-specific history lookup
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:5.1

Files:
- tests/unit/github-api.test.ts
- tests/unit/ (history-related coverage)
- src/shared/github-api.ts

Workflow mandate:
- Apply TDD: write failing tests for commit history loading and path-scoped lookups first.
- Keep this ticket focused on test definition, not feature completion.
- Defer cleanup to 6.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tests cover loading recent commits for a repository
- [ ] Tests cover path-specific history lookup from a selected artifact or document
- [ ] Tests specify expected error and empty-state behavior
- [ ] The failing cases are reproducible and isolated
close_reason: Added failing tests in tests/unit/github-api.test.ts for repository-wide commit history, path-specific history lookup, empty-state, cache separation, and history fetch errors. Confirmed RED state via TypeScript check showing missing GitHubClient.getCommitHistory API; bun is unavailable in this shell.

title: 5.2: History overview with recent commits and changed paths
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:5.2

Files:
- src/main.ts
- src/shared/github-api.ts
- src/shared/router.ts
- tests/unit/

Workflow mandate:
- Implement from the failing tests created in 5.1.
- Preserve repository context and integrate with the existing reader navigation.
- Leave structural cleanup for the tidy phase.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] A history overview shows recent commits and changed paths
- [ ] Automated tests cover the overview behavior
- [ ] Navigation into history mode preserves repository context
- [ ] Error or empty states are handled without breaking the reader
close_reason: Implemented OpenSpec task 5.2 by adding GitHubClient.getCommitHistory with session caching, history route support, a history overview renderer, and a first-class history screen reachable from repository overview. The reader now preserves repo context for history mode and handles recent commits, empty history, and API failures. Verified with bun test tests/unit/github-api.test.ts tests/unit/router.test.ts tests/unit/history-overview.test.ts and bun run build.

title: 4.2: .wai overview and grouped browsing

- [ ] Users can browse artifacts grouped by project, artifact type, or location
- [ ] Automated tests cover the primary browsing flows

title: 4.1: Failing tests for .wai detection and grouped artifact browsing

Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:4.1

Files:
- tests/unit/

Workflow mandate:

- [ ] Tests cover grouped browsing by project, artifact type, or location

title: 3.2: Focused dependency view beyond graph mode
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:3.2

Files:
- src/main.ts
- src/shared/router.ts
- src/shared/github-api.ts
- tests/unit/

Workflow mandate:
- Start from the failing tests created in 3.1.
- Implement the smallest useful focused dependency view that satisfies the tests.
- Keep refactoring limited; broader cleanup belongs in 6.1.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] At least one focused dependency inspection mode is implemented in addition to graph mode
- [ ] Behavior is covered by automated tests
- [ ] Repository context is preserved while navigating the focused view
- [ ] Existing graph mode behavior remains intact
close_reason: Implemented OpenSpec task 3.2 by adding beads graph/focus route support in src/shared/router.ts and a minimal beads screen in src/main.ts/src/index.html/src/styles.css. Repository overview now opens Issues in graph mode, focused routes preserve repo context, and missing dependencies show a fallback message without breaking navigation. Verified with bun test tests/unit/router.test.ts and bun run build.

title: 3.1: Failing tests for focused dependency inspection and missing dependency handling
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:3.1

Files:
- tests/unit/ (add or extend beads/dependency coverage)
- src/main.ts
- src/shared/router.ts

Workflow mandate:
- Apply TDD: write the failing test cases first.
- Keep implementation changes out of this ticket except minimal scaffolding required to express the failing behavior.
- Tidy First: defer structural cleanup to a separate refactor ticket.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Tests cover at least one focused dependency inspection mode beyond the current graph view
- [ ] Tests cover missing dependency handling and expected fallback behavior
- [ ] Test failures clearly describe intended UX and routing behavior
- [ ] OpenSpec reference add-unified-repo-reader:3.1 is reflected in the work
close_reason: Added RED tests in tests/unit/router.test.ts for focused beads dependency routes, selected-issue neighborhood parsing, missing-dependency fallback context, and graph fallback when no issue is selected. Confirmed failures with bun test; router does not yet support view=beads focus-mode routing. OpenSpec task 3.1 complete as the failing-test phase.

title: F6: Add aria-live regions for loading and dynamic content
description: Loading repository text appears/disappears without screen reader announcement. No aria-live or aria-busy attributes on any dynamic content region. Screen reader users have no feedback that content is loading or has changed.
notes: Fix: wrap loading indicator in aria-live=polite region. Add aria-busy to content containers during fetch. Consider announcing view transitions.

title: F5: Update document.title per view
description: document.title stays as 'atril' across all views. Screen readers cannot distinguish views, and browser tab/history entries are all identical. This is an accessibility barrier and a general usability issue.
notes: Fix: set document.title in the showScreen() function. Pattern: 'README.md — charly-vibes/atril — atril' for file view, 'History — charly-vibes/atril — atril' for history, etc.

title: F1: Make all interactive elements keyboard-accessible
description: WCAG 2.1 Level A violation (2.1.1 Keyboard). Interactive items (suggestion list items, tree items, breadcrumb segments) are div/li/span elements with click handlers but no tabindex, no role=button/link, and no keydown handlers for Enter/Space. Keyboard-only and screen-reader users cannot activate any content navigation.
notes: Affected elements: .suggestion-item (overview), .tree-item (tree view), .breadcrumb-seg (file/history nav), .wai-artifact-history (wai view), .changed-path-link (history). Fix: add tabindex=0, appropriate role, and keydown handler for Enter/Space to all clickable non-semantic elements.

title: Knowledge source detection and repo overview
description: After loading a repo, detect presence of openspec/, .beads/, .wai/, docs, and README. Render an overview dashboard with entry points for detected sources. Handle repos with no structured knowledge gracefully. openspec ref: add-unified-repo-reader:1.3
notes: Traceability note: primary implementation ticket for OpenSpec task 1.3, with validation overlap into task 1.4.
close_reason: Entry flow with validation, loading, error states. Overview detects openspec/beads/wai/docs/readme and suggests entry points. 18 new tests.

title: Repository entry flow with validation error display
description: Wire parseRepoInput into a full entry flow: on valid input, load the repo via GitHub API and transition to overview. On invalid input, show validation error. On API error, show error state. openspec ref: add-unified-repo-reader:1.2
notes: Traceability note: primary implementation ticket for OpenSpec task 1.2 (repository entry flow and error handling).
close_reason: Entry flow with validation, loading, error states. Overview detects openspec/beads/wai/docs/readme and suggests entry points. 18 new tests.

title: GitHub API client with session caching
description: Shared module for unauthenticated GitHub REST API calls. Must cache responses in-memory per session, handle rate limits, and detect truncated responses. Platform spec requires ~60 req/hr budget. Needed by all viewers. openspec ref: add-unified-repo-reader:1.2
notes: Traceability note: foundational API/client work supporting OpenSpec tasks 1.2-1.4; not itself a standalone user-visible task.
close_reason: GitHub API client implemented with session caching, rate limit handling, 404 errors, truncated tree detection. 10 tests passing.

title: Add issue search by title and description
description: Add a search input to the beads viewer that filters issues whose title or description contains the query text. Show empty state when no issues match. Per spec requirement 'Issue Search'.
close_reason: Added filterIssues() with status/type/priority/search support, toolbar with dropdowns and search input, event delegation for live filtering. 15 new tests for filter logic, 3 for toolbar rendering.

title: Wire focused dependency view with real issue data
description: Replace the focus-mode placeholder with actual data: show a selected issue with its direct blockers and dependents. Provide navigation back to the broader graph/list context without losing repository selection. Per delta spec 'Dependency Exploration Modes'.

title: Add issue filtering by status, type, and priority
description: Add filter controls to the issue list view: filter by status (open/closed/in_progress), type (task/bug/feature/epic), and priority (P0-P4). Filters should be combinable — applying multiple narrows results to the intersection. Per spec requirement 'Issue Filtering'.
close_reason: Added filterIssues() with status/type/priority/search support, toolbar with dropdowns and search input, event delegation for live filtering. 15 new tests for filter logic, 3 for toolbar rendering.

title: Implement interactive dependency graph visualization
description: Replace the graph-mode placeholder with an interactive dependency graph: issues as nodes, dependencies as directed edges. Support pan and zoom. Handle empty graphs (no dependencies), missing dependency references (graceful degradation), and dependency cycles (render without hanging). Per spec requirement 'Dependency Graph View'.

title: Implement issue list/detail view for beads viewer
description: Replace the graph-mode placeholder with a list/detail layout: sidebar showing browsable issues, main panel showing full metadata (title, description, status, type, priority, assignee, dependencies) when an issue is selected. This is the primary browsing interface per spec requirement 'List Detail View'.
close_reason: Implemented list/detail view: beads-renderer.ts (pure render), beads-loader.ts integration in main.ts, list mode as default in router, click delegation for issue selection and dependency links, responsive CSS layout, 12 renderer tests + 6 new router tests. All 182 tests pass.

title: Load and parse .beads/issues.jsonl from GitHub
description: Fetch .beads/issues.jsonl via GitHub API with branch fallback (default → beads-sync). Parse JSONL into typed issue objects. Handle missing file, malformed JSON, and empty file gracefully. This is the data foundation all other beads viewer features depend on.
close_reason: Added src/shared/beads-loader.ts with parseIssuesJsonl (pure JSONL parser) and loadBeadsIssues (branch fallback: default → beads-sync). Types: BeadsIssue, BeadsLoadResult. 11 tests covering parsing, malformed lines, branch fallback, empty files, and timestamps.

title: F9: Add focus management after view transitions
description: When switching views programmatically (e.g., overview → file, file → history), focus is not moved to the new content. Keyboard users are left at their previous position. Only autofocus on entry input and .focus() on error recovery are implemented.
notes: Fix: call .focus() on the new view's heading or first interactive element after showScreen(). Consider using a focus-management utility that restores focus on back navigation.

title: F4: Source badges look clickable but are not interactive
description: The overview source badges (SPECS, ISSUES, MEMORY, DOCS, README) appear as pill-like indicators. Users may try clicking them expecting navigation to the relevant view, but they are purely decorative span elements with data-active attributes.
notes: Either make them clickable links that navigate to the relevant view (Specs→tree filtered to openspec/, Issues→beads, Memory→wai, Docs→tree filtered to docs/, README→file README.md) or visually de-emphasize to avoid affordance mismatch.

title: F10: Beads graph view is a dead-end placeholder
description: Navigating to Issues from the overview shows only a static placeholder message ('Graph mode remains available... Use a focused dependency route...') with no interactive content. Users cannot browse issues without manually constructing a URL with mode=focus&issue=ID.
notes: Superseded by implementation tickets: atril-9ie (data loading) → atril-phn (list/detail) → atril-ax6 (filtering), atril-2ay (search), atril-j2f (URLs), atril-w01 (cross-refs). Graph track: atril-9ie → atril-bbm (graph) → atril-70w (focus). Also atril-8my (freshness).
close_reason: The beads graph view placeholder is no longer the default entry point. Users now land on a functional list/detail view with filtering, search, and issue selection. Graph and focus mode placeholders remain for future work (atril-bbm, atril-70w).

title: F2: Add favicon to prevent 404 console error
description: No favicon file or link rel=icon in HTML. Every page load triggers a 404 console error for /favicon.ico. Looks unfinished in browser tabs.
notes: Fix: add a simple SVG favicon inline via <link rel='icon' type='image/svg+xml' href='data:...'> or add a favicon.ico/png file to the build.

title: Add file navigation: browsable file tree and fuzzy search
description:
Currently the overview page only shows a few entry points (README, project.md, issues, wai, docs/). Individual spec files and deeper paths (e.g. openspec/specs/platform/spec.md, openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/specs/repo-overview/spec.md) are only reachable by manually constructing URLs or following inline links. Users need a way to discover and navigate to any file in the repo.

Proposed approach:
- Add a browsable file tree or searchable file index accessible from the overview
- Include fuzzy search (type-ahead) so users can quickly find files by partial name
- The repo tree data is already available (GitHub API getTree), just needs a UI surface
close_reason: Closed

title: 6.3: Strict OpenSpec validation for add-unified-repo-reader
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:6.3

Files:
- openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/tasks.md
- openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/proposal.md
- openspec/specs/

Workflow mandate:
- Run strict OpenSpec validation only after implementation and documentation are updated.
- Fix validation findings in the appropriate files without expanding scope.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] `openspec validate add-unified-repo-reader --strict` is run
- [ ] Validation findings are resolved or captured explicitly
- [ ] OpenSpec tasks and proposal artifacts are consistent with delivered work
- [ ] The change is ready for final review or archival steps

title: 6.2: Update README and supporting docs for unified repository reader UX
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:6.2

Files:
- README.md
- openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/proposal.md
- openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/tasks.md

Workflow mandate:
- Document the shipped UX after feature work is complete.
- Keep docs aligned with actual behavior and avoid speculative content.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] README describes the unified repository reader UX and supported modes
- [ ] Documentation covers repository entry, navigation, and known limits as needed
- [ ] Docs reflect the implemented behavior accurately
- [ ] OpenSpec task references remain consistent

title: 6.1: Tidy shared repository-context and routing abstractions
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:6.1

Files:
- src/shared/router.ts
- src/shared/link-resolver.ts
- src/shared/github-api.ts
- src/main.ts
- tests/unit/

Workflow mandate:
- This is a dedicated refactor ticket: no new feature scope.
- Use Tidy First to simplify shared repository-context and routing abstractions after the feature tests are green.
- Preserve behavior with automated tests.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Shared repository-context or routing abstractions are simplified without changing behavior
- [ ] Existing tests remain green or are improved to protect the refactor
- [ ] No new feature requirements are introduced in this ticket
- [ ] The codebase is in a cleaner state for documentation and validation work
close_reason: Extracted escapeHtml to shared module (4 duplicates removed), unified LinkStatus type, consolidated back-button handlers. 129 tests green, net -36 lines.

title: 5.3: Path-aware history from selected document or artifact view
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:5.3

Files:
- src/main.ts
- src/shared/router.ts
- src/shared/github-api.ts
- tests/unit/

Workflow mandate:
- Extend tests before wiring path-aware navigation.
- Reuse existing selection and routing abstractions where possible.
- Keep implementation and refactoring separate.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Users can open path-specific history starting from a selected document or artifact
- [ ] Automated tests cover path-aware navigation and lookup behavior
- [ ] Repository and path context remain intact across transitions
- [ ] Unsupported paths degrade gracefully
close_reason: Implemented path-aware history navigation from file view, WAI view, and history drill-down (OpenSpec 5.3)

title: 4.3: Readable rendering for .wai artifacts with preserved repository context
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:4.3

Files:
- src/main.ts
- src/shared/router.ts
- src/shared/link-resolver.ts
- tests/unit/

Workflow mandate:
- Add rendering-focused tests before implementation where coverage is missing.
- Preserve repository context and avoid introducing one-off viewers if existing document rendering can be extended.
- Defer cleanup to the tidy ticket.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] .wai artifacts render readably in the repository reader
- [ ] Repository context and navigation remain intact while reading artifacts
- [ ] Automated tests cover rendering and navigation expectations

close_reason: Closed

title: 3.3: Issue-to-artifact navigation for resolvable references
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:3.3

Files:
- src/main.ts
- src/shared/router.ts
- src/shared/link-resolver.ts
- tests/unit/

Workflow mandate:
- Add or extend tests before changing navigation behavior.
- Prefer reuse of existing repository-context and link-routing abstractions.
- Tidy First: isolate incidental cleanup from feature work.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Issues can navigate to resolvable artifacts when references are present
- [ ] Unsupported or unresolved references degrade gracefully
- [ ] Tests cover successful and unsuccessful navigation cases
- [ ] Repository context is preserved across navigation
close_reason: Implemented issue-to-artifact navigation with reference extraction, resolution, and graceful degradation. Reviewed via Ro5.

title: 2.2: Repository-aware link routing for document views
description: Implement repository-aware link routing for supported document views. Depends on 2.1 tests. OpenSpec task: add-unified-repo-reader:2.2
close_reason: Closed

title: 2.4: Graceful fallback for unresolved links
description: Verify graceful fallback for unresolved links and missing targets. OpenSpec task: add-unified-repo-reader:2.4
close_reason: Closed

title: 2.3: Surface related OpenSpec artifacts from spec documents
description: Surface related OpenSpec artifacts from spec documents without losing repository context. OpenSpec task: add-unified-repo-reader:2.3
close_reason: Closed

title: 2.1: Failing tests for link resolution and anchor navigation
description: Add failing tests for internal repository link resolution and anchor navigation. OpenSpec task: add-unified-repo-reader:2.1
close_reason: Closed

title: Add data freshness indicator to beads viewer
description: Display a timestamp showing when issue data was last fetched from GitHub, so users can assess data freshness. Per spec requirement 'Data Freshness Indicator'.
close_reason: Data freshness indicator already rendered in beads-renderer.ts — shows branch name and fetched-at date at top of list view.

title: Wire shareable URLs to actual issue selection
description: Ensure selecting an issue updates the URL with repo and issue ID as query params (consistent with spec-viewer ?repo= pattern). Opening a shared URL should load the repo, fetch issues, and select the referenced issue. Routing infrastructure exists but isn't connected to real data. Per spec requirement 'Shareable URLs'.
close_reason: Shareable URLs already work: router encodes mode=list&issue=<id> on selection, parseRoute recovers both. Selecting an issue updates URL via navigate(), loading a shared URL restores the selection.

title: Wire issue-to-artifact cross-reference navigation
description: Connect the existing issue-reference.ts resolution logic to the actual issue detail UI. When viewing an issue, display navigable links to related specs, change proposals, and docs. Show unresolvable references as plain text without broken links. Per delta spec 'Issue Artifact Cross-References'.
close_reason: Wired resolveIssueReferences into beads-renderer.ts detail view. Navigable links show as buttons, external as labeled, unresolvable as plain text. 3 new tests. The existing issue-ref-link click handler in main.ts already navigates to files.

title: F11: Inconsistent history item expand behavior
description: Some commit items in history view are expandable <details> elements showing author/date metadata, while others are simple clickable rows. The visual difference is subtle and unpredictable for users.
notes: Make all history items consistently expandable with author/date/changed-files, or remove expansion entirely for a uniform list.

title: F8: Wrap content area in main landmark element
description: Content area lacks a <main> element. Screen reader users using landmark navigation cannot jump directly to main content. Related to skip-to-content link implementation.
notes: Add <main id='content'> element wrapping the screen containers in index.html.

title: F7: Add skip-to-content link
description: No skip navigation link exists. Keyboard users must Tab through the theme toggle and navigation bar on every view before reaching main content.
notes:
Add Skip to content as first child of body, visually hidden until focused. Add
wrapper around content area.

title: F3: Verify and fix muted text color contrast
description: --color-muted: #807a72 (light) and #918b82 (dark) on surface backgrounds may fail WCAG AA 4.5:1 contrast ratio. Needs verification with automated tooling and adjustment if below threshold.
notes: Use Lighthouse or axe-core to measure exact contrast ratios. If failing, bump muted colors darker (light theme) or lighter (dark theme) until 4.5:1 is met.

title: 5.4: Validate caching and rate-limit handling for history mode
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:5.4

Validation target:
- Automated coverage in `tests/unit/github-api.test.ts` using mocked GitHub API responses for cache hits, cache misses, and rate-limit failures
- Manual smoke test against repository `charly-vibes/atril` for recent commit and path-specific history flows

Commands:
- bun test tests/unit/github-api.test.ts
- bun test
- bun run build
- Open the built app and load repo `charly-vibes/atril`
- Open history mode, inspect recent commits, then open path-specific history from a selected document or artifact
- Simulate or verify rate-limit handling through test fixtures rather than waiting for the live API limit

Evidence path:
- docs/validation/add-unified-repo-reader-5.4.md

Files:
- src/shared/github-api.ts
- tests/unit/github-api.test.ts
- README.md
- openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/tasks.md
- docs/validation/add-unified-repo-reader-5.4.md

Workflow mandate:
- Validate behavior under caching and GitHub API rate-limit constraints.
- Capture findings and follow-up defects explicitly; avoid mixing broad cleanup into this ticket.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] History mode behavior is validated under caching scenarios
- [ ] Rate-limit or API error handling is exercised and documented
- [ ] Automated or manual validation evidence is recorded
- [ ] OpenSpec task status can be updated from the validation result

title: 4.4: Manual validation for repositories without .wai
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:4.4

Validation target:
- Repository: octocat/Hello-World
- Use this repository as the missing-`.wai` case unless a smaller known public repo without `.wai` is documented before execution

Commands:
- bun test
- bun run build
- Open the built app and load repo `octocat/Hello-World`
- Confirm the repository overview does not advertise `.wai` browsing
- If a `.wai` route is entered directly, confirm the app shows a clear fallback or empty state instead of a broken view

Evidence path:
- docs/validation/add-unified-repo-reader-4.4.md

Files:
- README.md
- openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/tasks.md
- docs/validation/add-unified-repo-reader-4.4.md

Workflow mandate:
- Manually verify missing-.wai behavior after implementation.
- Record observed UX and any follow-up defects explicitly.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Manual validation confirms behavior on repositories without .wai
- [ ] Empty-state or fallback UX is documented
- [ ] Any discovered issues are captured as follow-up work
- [ ] OpenSpec task status can be updated from the validation results

title: 3.4: Manual validation for dense dependency graphs
description:
Context: OpenSpec task add-unified-repo-reader:3.4

Validation target:
- Repository: charly-vibes/atril
- Use the current repository's beads data as the dense dependency graph scenario

Commands:
- bun test
- bun run build
- Open the built app and load repo `charly-vibes/atril`
- Enter beads reading mode and inspect both the broad dependency graph and the focused dependency view
- Compare readability for issues with multiple blockers/dependents, including 6.1 and its neighbors

Evidence path:
- docs/validation/add-unified-repo-reader-3.4.md

Files:
- README.md
- openspec/changes/add-unified-repo-reader/tasks.md
- docs/validation/add-unified-repo-reader-3.4.md

Workflow mandate:
- Validate the implemented dependency reading UX against at least one dense graph scenario.
- Record findings and follow-up defects explicitly rather than folding them into silent code changes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Manual validation is performed on a repository or fixture with a dense dependency graph
- [ ] Readability findings and any defects are recorded
- [ ] OpenSpec task status can be updated based on the validation result

title: F12: Expand reference documentation beyond index
description: docs/reference/index.md acts as a pointer file rather than comprehensive reference. Missing coverage of URL query parameters, supported file types, API rate limits, and keyboard shortcuts.
notes: Add reference sections for: URL schema (?view=, ?owner=, ?repo=, ?branch=, ?path=, ?mode=, ?issue=, ?search=), supported rendering formats (.md, .org, plain text), GitHub API rate limit behavior (60/hr unauthenticated), theme persistence (localStorage).
